description: ATSPM-style signal-performance analytics derived from live event tables (phase_state_change, detector_transition) via ClickHouse window functions, tDigest percentiles, and sequence math. Purdue phase-termination breakdown, split-failure detection, green-duration percentiles, cycle length, detector occupancy, and vendor cohort comparison.

options.content:
## ATSPM-derived signal analytics, computed from raw controller events.

Every phase termination, detector activation, and green interval streams off the ASCs as high-resolution events. From that raw feed the pipeline derives the classic ATSPM measures — Purdue phase-termination diagrams, split-failure detection, green-duration percentiles, and a cross-vendor cohort comparison — with no vendor's proprietary ATSPM module in the loop.

panels.description: Per phase, share of YELLOW-onset terminations (the moment termination_reason is meaningful) that were MAX_OUT vs GAP_OUT. Heavy MAX_OUT in a phase signals undersized split timing. Note: stacking is 'normal' not 'percent' intentionally — percent-stacking mis-scales the percent-unit axis in this plugin.

targets.rawSql: SELECT concat('phase ', toString(phase_number)) AS phase, round(countIf(termination_reason='TERMINATION_REASON_GAP_OUT')*100.0/nullIf(countIf(termination_reason IN ('TERMINATION_REASON_MAX_OUT','TERMINATION_REASON_GAP_OUT')),0),1) AS gap_out_pct, round(countIf(termination_reason='TERMINATION_REASON_MAX_OUT')*100.0/nullIf(countIf(termination_reason IN ('TERMINATION_REASON_MAX_OUT','TERMINATION_REASON_GAP_OUT')),0),1) AS max_out_pct FROM vikasa_${dot}.phase_state_change WHERE $__timeFilter(ce_time) AND to_state='TO_STATE_YELLOW' AND cabinet_id IN ($cabinet) GROUP BY phase_number ORDER BY phase_number

panels.title: Purdue phase-termination matrix

panels.description: Top-15 cabinet x phase combinations by MAX_OUT% share, ranked worst-first, among those with >=5 YELLOW terminations in the window (statistical-significance gate). Real traffic engineers add demand to those phases or shorten the cycle.

targets.rawSql: SELECT cabinet_id, phase_number AS phase, countIf(termination_reason='TERMINATION_REASON_MAX_OUT') AS max_outs, countIf(termination_reason IN ('TERMINATION_REASON_MAX_OUT','TERMINATION_REASON_GAP_OUT')) AS terminations, round(countIf(termination_reason='TERMINATION_REASON_MAX_OUT')*100.0/nullIf(countIf(termination_reason IN ('TERMINATION_REASON_MAX_OUT','TERMINATION_REASON_GAP_OUT')),0),1) AS max_out_pct FROM vikasa_${dot}.phase_state_change WHERE $__timeFilter(ce_time) AND to_state='TO_STATE_YELLOW' AND cabinet_id IN ($cabinet) GROUP BY cabinet_id, phase HAVING terminations >= 5 ORDER BY max_out_pct DESC LIMIT 15

panels.title: Split-failure suspects

panels.description: Network-wide green-interval percentiles per phase, pooled across all cabinets in the $cabinet selection (defaults to the whole DOT). Consistent p50/p90 across the network means the corridor is timed uniformly; a phase with a long tail flags outlier cabinets to drill into.

targets.rawSql: SELECT concat('phase ', toString(phase_number)) AS phase, round(quantileTDigest(0.5)(green_s), 1) AS p50_s, round(quantileTDigest(0.9)(green_s), 1) AS p90_s FROM (SELECT cabinet_id, phase_number, arrayJoin(arrayMap((t, s, tn, sn) -> (s = 'TO_STATE_GREEN' AND sn = 'TO_STATE_YELLOW' ? dateDiff('millisecond', t, tn) / 1000.0 : -1), arr_t, arr_s, arrayPushBack(arraySlice(arr_t, 2), toDateTime64(0, 3)), arrayPushBack(arraySlice(arr_s, 2), ''))) AS green_s FROM (SELECT cabinet_id, phase_number, groupArray((ce_time, to_state)) AS pairs, arraySort(x -> x.1, pairs) AS sorted, arrayMap(x -> x.1, sorted) AS arr_t, arrayMap(x -> x.2, sorted) AS arr_s FROM (SELECT cabinet_id, phase_number, ce_time, to_state FROM vikasa_${dot}.phase_state_change WHERE $__timeFilter(ce_time) AND to_state IN ('TO_STATE_GREEN','TO_STATE_YELLOW') AND cabinet_id IN ($cabinet)) GROUP BY cabinet_id, phase_number)) WHERE green_s BETWEEN 1 AND 120 GROUP BY phase_number ORDER BY phase_number

panels.title: Green-duration percentiles (p50 / p90) by phase — network-wide

panels.description: Successive phase-2 GREEN onsets per cabinet via lagInFrame window function — a proxy for cycle length. QUALIFY bounds out startup/gap artifacts.

targets.rawSql: SELECT cabinet_id, ce_time AS time, dateDiff('second', lagInFrame(ce_time) OVER (PARTITION BY cabinet_id ORDER BY ce_time), ce_time) AS cycle_s FROM vikasa_${dot}.phase_state_change WHERE $__timeFilter(ce_time) AND to_state='TO_STATE_GREEN' AND phase_number=2 AND cabinet_id IN ($cabinet) QUALIFY cycle_s BETWEEN 30 AND 300 ORDER BY time

panels.title: Cycle length · phase-2 GREEN onset deltas

panels.description: Mean ON-state duration per detector channel (paired ON -> next event via leadInFrame window). Approach/lane/phase_served are per-event dims but channel is used as the aggregation grain.

targets.rawSql: SELECT cabinet_id, channel, round(avg(dur),2) AS avg_on_seconds, count() AS intervals FROM (SELECT cabinet_id, channel, transition, ce_time, dateDiff('second', ce_time, leadInFrame(ce_time) OVER (PARTITION BY cabinet_id, channel ORDER BY ce_time ASC ROWS BETWEEN CURRENT ROW AND 1 FOLLOWING)) AS dur FROM vikasa_${dot}.detector_transition WHERE $__timeFilter(ce_time) AND cabinet_id IN ($cabinet)) WHERE transition='TRANSITION_ON' AND dur > 0 AND dur < 600 GROUP BY cabinet_id, channel ORDER BY avg_on_seconds DESC LIMIT 50

panels.title: Detector occupancy · avg ON seconds by channel

panels.description: YELLOW-onset terminations per 15-minute bucket, stacked by termination_reason. A rising MAX_OUT band under steady volume signals split timing drifting undersized.

targets.rawSql: SELECT toStartOfInterval(ce_time, INTERVAL 15 MINUTE) AS time, termination_reason AS reason, count() AS terminations FROM vikasa_${dot}.phase_state_change WHERE $__timeFilter(ce_time) AND to_state='TO_STATE_YELLOW' AND cabinet_id IN ($cabinet) GROUP BY time, reason ORDER BY time

panels.title: Termination reason over time · 15-min buckets

panels.description: Detector ON activations by channel x 1-minute bucket. Banding shows the fixture's per-phase detector pulse pattern; spikes are concurrent demand.

targets.rawSql: SELECT toStartOfMinute(ce_time) AS time, channel, count() AS activations FROM vikasa_${dot}.detector_transition WHERE $__timeFilter(ce_time) AND transition='TRANSITION_ON' AND cabinet_id IN ($cabinet) GROUP BY time, channel ORDER BY time, channel

panels.title: Detector activations · channel x 1-min bucket

panels.description: Gap-out/max-out share grouped by vendor via JOIN to the cabinets dimension table (never inferred via LIKE). Drift between cohorts is a leading indicator of firmware divergence.

targets.rawSql: SELECT CASE WHEN c.vendor = 'econolite' THEN 'Econolite ASC3' ELSE 'McCain MaxTime' END AS vendor, count(DISTINCT p.cabinet_id) AS cabinets, count() AS phase_events, round(countIf(p.termination_reason='TERMINATION_REASON_GAP_OUT')*100.0/nullIf(countIf(p.termination_reason IN ('TERMINATION_REASON_GAP_OUT','TERMINATION_REASON_MAX_OUT')),0),1) AS gap_out_pct, round(countIf(p.termination_reason='TERMINATION_REASON_MAX_OUT')*100.0/nullIf(countIf(p.termination_reason IN ('TERMINATION_REASON_GAP_OUT','TERMINATION_REASON_MAX_OUT')),0),1) AS max_out_pct FROM vikasa_${dot}.phase_state_change p JOIN vikasa_${dot}.cabinets c ON p.cabinet_id = c.cabinet_id WHERE $__timeFilter(p.ce_time) AND p.to_state='TO_STATE_YELLOW' AND p.cabinet_id IN ($cabinet) GROUP BY vendor ORDER BY phase_events DESC

panels.title: Vendor cohort comparison

list.label: Cabinet
list.name: cabinet
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT cabinet_id FROM vikasa_${dot}.cabinets ORDER BY 1

title: Signal Performance (ATSPM)
